metadata.assessed_by: AWS Transform Custom — Agentic Readiness Assessment
metadata.archetype_justification: HAPI FHIR provides a full JPA/Hibernate persistence layer with CRUD operations on FHIR resources, database migrations, and entity lifecycle management.
metadata.context: Open-source Java implementation of the HL7 FHIR healthcare standard.

classification.rule_matched: 0 High, ≥2 Medium, ≥3 safety-impact Medium → Pilot-Ready (Safety Concerns)

findings.title: Scoped Permissions (Least Privilege) — Framework-level support only
findings.description: HAPI FHIR provides a comprehensive AuthorizationInterceptor with RuleBuilder supporting fine-grained rules. However, no IAM policies, API Gateway resource policies, or platform-level least-privilege enforcement are defined in this repository.
findings.gap: No infrastructure-as-code defining IAM roles, API Gateway policies, or platform-level permission scoping for agent identities.
findings.recommendation: Create deployment templates (IaC) defining agent-specific IAM roles with explicit action-level permissions integrated with the existing AuthorizationInterceptor.

compensating_controls: Deploy with API Gateway authorizers that enforce agent-specific IAM roles with read-only resource policies
Configure AuthorizationInterceptor rules at deployment time to limit agent identities to specific FHIR resource types and operations

findings.title: Immutable Audit Logging — Audit events stored in mutable datastore
findings.description: HAPI FHIR provides BALP interceptor generating AuditEvent resources and Hibernate Envers for entity-level audit trails. Audit events stored in same mutable database. No immutable log storage configured.
findings.gap: Audit events generated but stored in mutable datastore. No immutable, tamper-evident audit log storage.
findings.recommendation: Implement an IAuditDataStore that writes to an immutable external store (S3 with Object Lock or CloudWatch Logs with retention policy).

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, downgraded from conditional BLOCKER
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: AUTH-Q6 evaluated as RISK-SAFETY because agent_scope is read-only. Audit logging remains important but is not a deployment blocker.
compensating_controls: Forward AuditEvent resources to an external immutable log sink
Deploy with AWS CloudTrail enabled for API-level audit trail

findings.title: Agent Identity Suspension — No built-in revocation mechanism
findings.description: HAPI FHIR does not include built-in mechanisms for suspending or revoking individual agent identities. BearerTokenAuthInterceptor validates tokens but provides no revocation interface.
findings.gap: No mechanism to immediately suspend or revoke a specific agent identity without impacting other consumers.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with an external identity provider supporting immediate per-client credential revocation.

compensating_controls: Use an external identity provider (Cognito, Okta) with per-client disable capability
Deploy behind API Gateway with per-API-key revocation

findings.title: Circuit Breakers and Resilience — Retry only, no circuit breaker
findings.description: Spring Retry with exponential backoff exists but no formal circuit breaker pattern. External calls to terminology servers and subscription endpoints lack circuit breaker protection.
findings.gap: No circuit breaker implementation. Cascading failures from external dependencies not prevented.
findings.recommendation: Add Resilience4j circuit breaker annotations on methods calling external terminology servers and subscription endpoints.

compensating_controls: Add Resilience4j circuit breaker at deployment for external service calls
Configure timeout limits on HTTP client connections to external services

findings.title: Rate Limiting and Throttling — No production rate limiter
findings.description: No production rate limiting middleware. Only TransactionConcurrencySemaphoreInterceptor for concurrent transaction bundle processing.
findings.gap: No per-client or per-endpoint rate limiting to prevent runaway agent loops.
findings.recommendation: Deploy with API Gateway usage plans defining per-agent throttle limits.

compensating_controls: Deploy behind AWS API Gateway with usage plans and throttle settings
Add WAF rate-based rules at the infrastructure layer

findings.title: Data Residency and Sovereignty — No residency enforcement for healthcare PHI
findings.description: HAPI FHIR stores healthcare data (PHI) subject to HIPAA, GDPR. Multi-tenancy supported but no data residency enforcement implemented.
findings.gap: No data residency enforcement. Healthcare PHI with no controls preventing agent transmission to LLM endpoints in other jurisdictions.
findings.recommendation: Deploy with region-locked infrastructure. Implement data classification identifying fields requiring residency controls.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, downgraded from conditional BLOCKER
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: DATA-Q2 evaluated as RISK-SAFETY because agent_scope is read-only. Residency still relevant but lower risk when no data modification occurs.
compensating_controls: Deploy with region-locked infrastructure ensuring LLM endpoints co-located with data
Implement VPC endpoints to Amazon Bedrock in same AWS region

findings.title: PII Redaction in Logs — No PHI scrubbing in logging pipeline
findings.description: LoggingInterceptor with configurable templates can include request headers, parameters, and response bodies. No built-in PII/PHI scrubbing or log redaction middleware.
findings.gap: Healthcare data in request/response bodies can leak into application logs.
findings.recommendation: Implement a custom logging interceptor that scrubs PHI. Integrate with Amazon Macie for log-level PII detection.

compensating_controls: Configure LoggingInterceptor to log only request metadata without body content
Deploy with CloudWatch log filters that mask PHI patterns

findings.title: Machine-Readable API Specification — Dynamic generation only
findings.description: OpenApiInterceptor auto-generates OpenAPI 3.x specs at runtime. No static OpenAPI spec committed to repository.
findings.gap: No static, version-controlled OpenAPI specification for offline agent tool generation.
findings.recommendation: Add a build step that exports the generated OpenAPI spec as a static artifact.

compensating_controls: Export generated OpenAPI spec as a build artifact
Use FHIR CapabilityStatement as canonical interface contract

findings.title: Structured Error Responses — Missing retryable signal
findings.description: All errors converted to FHIR OperationOutcome with severity, code, diagnostics. No explicit retryable boolean.
findings.gap: Missing retryable indicator. Agents must infer from HTTP status codes.
findings.recommendation: Add retryable extension to OperationOutcome responses.

compensating_controls: Document mapping from OperationOutcome issue codes to retryable/terminal
Agents infer retryability from HTTP status codes

findings.title: Credential Management — No secrets management integration
findings.description: No built-in secrets management (Vault, Secrets Manager). Credentials via Spring properties or environment variables. @SensitiveNoDisplay prevents serialization but no rotation.
findings.gap: No secrets management system integration. No rotation support.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with AWS Secrets Manager via Spring Cloud AWS for credential management with automatic rotation.

compensating_controls: Configure Spring Boot to read secrets from AWS Secrets Manager
Use environment variables injected from secrets store at deployment time

findings.title: Sandbox/Staging Environment — No dedicated staging config
findings.description: Spring Boot auto-configuration allows local testing with in-memory databases. Sample apps exist. No dedicated sandbox with production-equivalent data shape.
findings.gap: No staging environment with production-equivalent data for safe agent testing.
findings.recommendation: Create Docker Compose-based staging with synthetic FHIR data (Synthea-generated patients).

compensating_controls: Use Spring Boot with H2 in-memory database for local agent testing
Create Docker Compose environment with sample FHIR data

findings.title: System of Record Designations — No master data management
findings.description: No system-of-record designations or master data management processes built in. FHIR meta.source available but not enforced.
findings.gap: No golden record pattern. Agents querying multiple systems encounter conflicting data.
findings.recommendation: Configure FHIR resource provenance tracking. Implement HAPI FHIR MDM module.

compensating_controls: Use FHIR resource meta.source to identify originating system
Implement MDM module for patient matching

findings.title: Schema Versioning and API Contracts — No CI breaking change detection
findings.description: Multi-version FHIR support with content negotiation. No breaking-change detection tooling in CI.
findings.gap: No automated contract testing in CI. Breaking API changes not automatically detected.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec diff or Pact contract tests to CI pipeline.

compensating_controls: FHIR specification versioning provides strong stability guarantees
CapabilityStatement declares supported operations as contract

findings.title: Distributed Tracing and Structured Logging — Partial implementation
findings.description: OpenTelemetry dependency and MDC usage present. No trace context propagation or structured JSON logging built-in.
findings.gap: No trace context propagation. No JSON structured logging. No consistent correlation_id.
findings.recommendation: Add server interceptor propagating traceparent headers and JSON logging via Logback encoder.

compensating_controls: Configure Logback JSON encoder at deployment
Add OpenTelemetry Java agent for automatic trace propagation

findings.title: Alerting on Error Rates and Latency — No alerting defined
findings.description: No alerting configuration in repository. Performance logging exists but no threshold alerting.
findings.recommendation: Add IaC defining CloudWatch alarms for 5xx error rates and p99 latency.

compensating_controls: Configure CloudWatch alarms on API Gateway metrics at deployment
Use AWS X-Ray with anomaly detection

findings.title: Infrastructure Governance — No IaC in repository
findings.description: No infrastructure-as-code. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm, or Kustomize files found.
findings.gap: Agent-facing integration surface not defined as code.
findings.recommendation: Create deployment IaC defining API Gateway, IAM roles, VPC, database, and secrets management.

compensating_controls: Create separate IaC repository for deployment infrastructure
Use CDK or Terraform for deployment stack

findings.title: CI/CD with API Contract Testing — No contract testing
findings.description: Extensive CI/CD with parallel testing, CodeQL, Codecov. No API contract testing (Pact, Spring Cloud Contract, OpenAPI diff).
findings.gap: Breaking API changes not automatically detected in CI.
findings.recommendation: Add consumer-driven contract tests to CI pipeline.

compensating_controls: 313+ REST API integration tests provide significant coverage
FHIR specification compliance tests serve as de facto contract tests

findings.title: Rollback Capability — No deployment rollback defined
findings.description: No blue/green, canary, or CodeDeploy rollback mechanism. Release workflow handles versioning only.
findings.gap: No rollback strategy for broken agent-facing APIs.
findings.recommendation: Define deployment IaC with blue/green or canary deployment and automatic rollback triggers.

compensating_controls: Maven Central versioning allows consumers to pin previous versions
Deployment-level rollback at infrastructure layer

findings.title: Encryption at Rest — No encryption configuration defined
findings.description: No encryption-at-rest configuration. Library delegates entirely to infrastructure. No kms_key_id references.
findings.gap: No encryption defined. Healthcare PHI may be unencrypted without infrastructure configuration.
findings.recommendation: Define IaC with KMS encryption on all data stores (RDS, S3, EBS).

compensating_controls: Configure RDS/Aurora encryption with KMS at deployment
Enable EBS encryption for all volumes
